summary.intent: Add a new community testimonial from GitHub discussion #680 to the Moltis website, then make the testimonials section horizontally scrollable to accommodate future additions without increasing page height.
summary.outcome: Added Cstewart-HC's quote as a second community card with GitHub attribution, converted the testimonials section to a horizontal scroll container with snap behavior, and regenerated all 9 translated HTML files.

repo: The website uses a build script (website/scripts/build-i18n.mjs) to generate translated HTML files from index.en.html — changes to the English template must be followed by a rebuild to propagate to all locales.
Community quotes/testimonials are NOT included in the i18n translation strings — they remain in English across all locales, simplifying quote additions.
The existing scrollbar utility class is named `hide-scrollbar`, not `scrollbar-hide` — easy to get wrong when adding scroll containers.

code.finding: Community quotes section uses orange cards with quote icons; cards needed `flex-shrink-0` and fixed widths (w-72 sm:w-80) to work correctly in a horizontal scroll container.
Horizontal scroll container uses `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar snap-x snap-mandatory` with `snap-start` on each card for mobile snap behavior.

workflow: When adding quotes from GitHub discussions, fetch both the discussion content and author metadata separately to get attribution details.
After editing index.en.html, always run the i18n build script to regenerate all translated files — 10 files total (en + 9 locales).

friction: Initial attempt used `scrollbar-hide` class which doesn't exist in the codebase — had to grep to discover the correct class name is `hide-scrollbar`.
The i18n translation system required a separate rebuild step after every HTML edit, adding overhead to what might seem like simple content changes.
open_items: The horizontal scroll container has no visual affordance (arrows, dots, fade edge) to indicate more cards exist — users may not discover the scroll on desktop.
Adding more quotes in the future requires manually duplicating card divs in index.en.html and re-running the i18n build — no CMS or data-driven approach exists yet.
